title: Hooks, Quality Gates, Signing, and Repository Security · Recovery Case: Safe Directory Ownership
summary: Add useful local feedback without pretending client-side hooks enforce server policy or executing untrusted repository code silently. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git safe directory ownership increment in a bounded Git automation and trust policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Respond to ownership and safe-directory warnings without broadly trusting unrelated repositories.
Treat hooks, filters, diff drivers, submodules, config includes, and build scripts as executable trust boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Safe Directory Ownership
steps.instruction:
theory case — A cloned repository proposes a pre-commit hook, diff driver, and build script. Determine what Git runs automatically, what is bypassable, and which server or CI gate owns enforcement before trust.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Safe Directory Ownership contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for respond to ownership and safe-directory warnings without broadly trusting unrelated repositories.

content.text: A cloned repository proposes a pre-commit hook, diff driver, and build script. Determine what Git runs automatically, what is bypassable, and which server or CI gate owns enforcement before trust.

content.text: Respond to ownership and safe-directory warnings without broadly trusting unrelated repositories. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A cloned repository proposes a pre-commit hook, diff driver, and build script. Determine what Git runs automatically, what is bypassable, and which server or CI gate owns enforcement before trust. A defensible solution applies Safe Directory Ownership, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a bounded Git automation and trust policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: changed Git case 5
# hypothesis: the mismatch is isolated to one repository layer
git status --short --branch
git diff
git diff --staged
git log --graph --decorate --oneline -n 6
# observation: name worktree, index, ref, and graph evidence separately
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Adding wildcard safe.directory is the correct fix for every ownership warning. This fails the Safe Directory Ownership contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
